items.summary: Equal-weighted S&P 500 is unchanged since war broke out.

items.title: ECB Chief Says Long-Term Inflation Expectations Remain On Target
items.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stressed that long-term inflation expectations are still broadly in line with the 2% target, despite deepening fallout from the Iran war.

items.title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth Slowed Before War Drove Inflation Up
items.summary: A key measure of euro-area pay growth slowed, providing some relief to European Central Bank officials worrying about emerging inflation risks from the Iran war.

items.title: U . S . Missile Shield Strain in Israel War Raises Global Alarm

We continue our defensive operations in the designated areas of active operations. Today, there was a report by Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of Ukraine Oleksandr Syrskyi on the use of long-range drones against Russian oil refining and export assets. In particular, overnight, the Defense Forces of Ukraine operated against targets associated with the Yaroslavl oil refinery – about 700 kilometers from our territory. We are bringing the war back home – to Russia – and that’s only fair. 

There were also strikes on designated targets in the temporarily occupied territory of Ukraine. We are also preparing other forms of our long-range sanctions and midrange strikes in response to Russian attacks on our cities and communities. 

We discussed in detail with the Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of Ukraine the frontline activity. Since the beginning of 2026, Russian losses on the front have already amounted to more than 145,000 personnel – including nearly 86,000 killed, at least 59,000 seriously wounded, and more than 800 Russian servicemembers taken prisoner. In the border areas of the Sumy region, we are achieving the designated objectives. We continue destroying Russian personnel and the occupiers’ equipment in other sectors as well. I especially want to thank the operators of our drones for their precision. Active operations are also ongoing. The Airborne Assault Forces and assault units conducting these operations deserve special recognition. I thank every Ukrainian unit that is achieving results!

items.title: Estee Lauder-Puig Talks Collapse
items.summary: The proposed multibillion-dollar tie-up between Puig and Estee Lauder fell apart due to makeup artist Charlotte Tilbury’s demands regarding her compensation in the deal, according to people familiar with the matter. The news caught investors by surprise given discussions between the two had been progressing since March. Bloomberg Opinion's Andrea Felsted has more. Her opinions are her own. (Source

items.title: US pauses $14bn weapons sale to Taiwan due to Iran war
items.summary: US acting navy chief Hung Cao told a Senate hearing they were "doing a pause" on the sale to make sure they had munitions for the Iran war.

items.title: Qatar Airways to Skip Staff Bonuses Due to Iran War Impact
items.summary: Qatar Airways QCSC will skip handing out bonuses to almost 60,000 workers this year after the US-Israel war on Iran forced the carrier to cancel tens of thousands of flights and lose out on billions in revenue.

items.title: Japan to Receive First Oil Tanker to Exit Hormuz Since War Began
items.summary: Japan — one of Asia’s biggest importers of energy from the Middle East — flagged the impending arrival of the first Persian Gulf oil shipment to transit the contested Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began.

items.title: EU’s Dombrovskis Sees ECB Rate Response to Inflation Shock
items.summary: European Union economy chief Valdis Dombrovskis said the European Central Bank will need to address the continent’s rising inflation due to the war in Iran.
